chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/322)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي جُرْمُوقٍ وَ أَتَيْتُهُ بِجُرْمُوقٍ فَبَعَثْتُ بِهِ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ يُصَلَّى فِيهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ibrahim Bin Mahziyar who said,
‘I asked himasws about the Salāt in a Jurmuq (gaiter – a large slipper), and I came with a gaiter and showed it to himasws. So heasws said: ‘One can pray Salāt in it’.
en.sarwar: 32. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ibrahim ibn Mahziyar who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a Jurmuq (a larger slipper worn over another one) if it is permissible to perform Salah (prayer) with it and I showed it to him (the Imam) and he (the Imam) said, ‘It is permissible to perform Salah (prayer) with it.’”
